title: The Risk Register
description:
The board wants a risk register on the screen at 09:05, right after the chair's opening remarks. You have five slots. Each slot is one risk and must contain:

  •  A risk statement — one sentence. What could fail, under what condition, with what consequence.
  •  A likelihood score (1–5, with justification).
  •  A business impact score (1–5, anchored in ₦, customer count, or regulatory exposure).
  •  The single control that, if implemented, would most reduce this risk — named concretely (a product, a policy, a process — not a platitude).
  •  The accountable owner by role — not by name.

Scoring: vague controls lose 4 pts each ('improve monitoring' is vague — 'deploy Wazuh agents on 100% of in-scope endpoints with 30-day retention' is concrete). Risks not traceable to evidence from Stages 1-3 lose 8 pts each. Use of the words 'robust', 'holistic', or 'synergies' is an automatic zero.

Structure your answer as five numbered blocks. Use numerals for scores. Be brief — each risk fits inside 90 words.

widgetConfig.placeholder:
1. Risk: ...
   Likelihood: 4/5 — ...
   Impact:     5/5 — ...
   Control:    ...
   Owner:      Head of Security Engineering

2. ...
